name: ui_module_definition
purpose: Design UI modules and pages for the application — derived from user tasks, business functions, and domain entities. Produces a structured UI module catalog and per-module page specifications.

modules.name: string
modules.purpose: one sentence — user task this module supports
backendModules: M-xx — backend module IDs this UI module depends on

pages.name: string
pages.purpose: what the user achieves on this page
drillsInto: PAGE-nn — pages reachable from this page

rules: Design from user tasks, not database tables
Every page must have an explicit purpose linked to a business function
Prefer dashboard, list, detail, and form page patterns
Keep navigation shallow — maximum two levels deep
Each drill-down relationship between pages must be explicit in drillsInto and navigation.flows
primaryUsers must reference actors defined in application.json
backendModules must reference module IDs from module-catalog.json
relatedEntities must reference entity names from entity files
Priority: core = required for MVP, secondary = important but deferrable, optional = nice-to-have
Filters only where data volume or search need is identified in requirements
Actions must include navigatesTo when they change the current page
No new requirements — all pages must be traceable to business functions or requirements
